description:
The crate-level docs mention the MAX_NV_BUFFER_SIZE limitation (lines 38-39: 'typically 1024-2048 bytes, TPM-dependent'), but put() has no doc comment on the impl method itself. The size limit is TPM-hardware-dependent and not discoverable programmatically.

When data exceeds MaxNvBuffer capacity, callers get SecretError::Backend with message 'data too large for NV index: ...' — a reasonable error, but the contract doesn't let callers know the limit beforehand or distinguish 'too large' from other Backend errors programmatically.

What goes wrong: A caller writing a 4KB secret to TPM NV gets a generic Backend error with no way to handle it differently from e.g. an auth failure. They have no way to query the limit before calling put().

Location: lib.rs line 337 (MaxNvBuffer::try_from), and missing doc comment on the put() impl (line 314).
title: WritableSecretStore::put() NV size limit undocumented on impl
